channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Five years since its inception, a US development agency competes with China on global projects
item.description: Since its inception in 2019, a U.S. development agency has created a portfolio of more than $50 billion worth of projects in 114 countries, including a trans-Africa railway corridor, a power plant in Sierra Leone, and solar panel manufacturing in India.
item.dc:creator: Didi Tang, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Florida State Rep. Susan Valdes, representing parts of Hillsborough County, switches political parties
item.description: State Rep. Susan Valdes, who represents Florida's 64th District, including parts of Hillsborough County, switched political parties, the Florida House of Representatives announced Monday.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump voices support for RFK Jr's vaccine views, suggests Kennedy will research debunked autism link
item.description: “When you look at some of the problems, when you look at what’s going on with disease and sickness in our country, something’s wrong,” Trump said on "Meet The Press.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, NewsNation

item.title: House Republican eyes Medicare, Social Security for DOGE cuts
item.description: Rep. Mark Alford (R-Mo.) is floating Medicare and Social Security as potential areas of focus for President-elect Trump’s new Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE).
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: Trump has flip-flopped on abortion policy. His appointees may offer clues to what happens next
item.description: As Donald Trump’s Cabinet begins to take shape, the nominees offer a preview of how his administration might handle abortion after he repeatedly flip-flopped on the issue on the campaign trail.
item.dc:creator: Christine Fernando, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Tulsi Gabbard heads to the Hill with Syria in spotlight
item.description: Gabbard — who served in the House for eight years as a Democrat — has drawn scrutiny for a 2017 trip she made to Syria to visit with Bashar al-Assad.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Top Dem says Congress should 'abandon' $895B defense bill over transgender treatment ban for kids
item.description: House Rep. Adam Smith, the top Democrat on the Armed Services Committee, is signaling he could withhold support from this year's defense policy bill.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Supreme Court turns away Boston school admissions policy challenge as Alito, Thomas dissent
item.description: The Supreme Court turned away a group of white and Asian parents’ challenge to a temporary admissions policy at Boston’s prestigious “exam schools” over dissents from two of the court’s conservatives.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: TikTok asks federal appeals court to bar enforcement of potential ban until Supreme Court review
item.description: TikTok on Monday asked a federal appeals court to bar the Biden administration from enforcing a law that could lead to a ban on the popular platform until the Supreme Court reviews the case.
item.dc:creator: Haleluya Hadero, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Democrat Ritchie Torres' torrent of attacks against own party fuels primary showdown buzz in New York
item.description: Rep. Ritchie Torres is considering a run for governor, his spokesperson said — and he's generating buzz by going after fellow Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump names Alina Habba as counselor to the president; reveals several State Department picks
item.description: President-elect Trump announced several nominations and appointments on Sunday, including his attorney Alina Habba, who will serve as counselor to the president.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump taps his attorney Alina Habba to serve as counselor to the president
item.description: FORT LAUDERDALE, Fla. (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump has announced that he is appointing one of his defense attorneys in the New York hush money case as counselor to the president. Alina Habba, 40, defended Trump earlier this year, also serving as his legal spokesperson.
item.dc:creator: ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump names Alina Habba as counselor to president
item.description: “Alina has been a tireless advocate for Justice, a fierce Defender of the Rule of Law, and an invaluable Advisor to my Campaign and Transition Team,” Trump said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Assad's fall in Syria is a 'fundamental act of justice,' but also 'a moment of risk,' Biden says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden said Sunday that the sudden collapse of the Syrian government under Bashar Assad is a “fundamental act of justice” after decades of repression, but it was “a moment of risk and uncertainty” for the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Democrats say they'll avoid election challenges on Jan. 6
item.description: House Democrats say they’ll skip the election protests they’ve staged on Jan. 6 in past presidential cycles, four years after supporters of President-elect Trump stormed the Capitol in an attempt to interrupt the certification of the 2020 election results.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, NewsNation

item.title: Trump offers a public show of support for Hegseth and says he believes he can be confirmed
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump on Friday offered a public show of support for Pete Hegseth, his embattled choice to lead the Defense Department, whose confirmation by the Senate is in doubt as he faces questions over allegations of excessive drinking, sexual assault and his views on women in combat.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN LONG and JILL COLVIN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Former national security officials urge caution on Gabbard
item.description: A group of nearly 100 former national security officials urged the Senate to “carefully scrutinize” former Rep. Tulsi Gabbard (Hawaii), President-elect Trump’s nominee to serve as director of national intelligence.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Johnson woos his sharpest critics in preparation for Speaker vote
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) is wooing his sharpest critics in the House GOP ahead of his official election for Speaker on the House floor, working to eliminate opposition and secure the strongest leadership mandate possible in a razor-thin majority. In the latest development, Rep.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Minneapolis City Council member Emily Koski joins mayor’s race
item.description: She joins Minnesota DFL Sen. Omar Fateh and the Rev. DeWayne Davis of Plymouth Congregational Church in challenging Mayor Jacob Frey, who’s running for reelection.
item.dc:creator: Megan Burks, MPR News

item.title: Pete Hegseth’s mother says The New York Times made ‘threats’ by asking her to comment on a story
item.description: A basic tenet of journalism — calling someone for comment on a story — was seen as a threat by defense secretary nominee Pete Hegseth’s mother.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump reportedly considering DeSantis as replacement nominee for defense secretary
item.description: Several reports, including from the Wall Street Journal, have said Governor Ron DeSantis could become President-elect Trump's new defense secretary nominee. The news comes amid a growing number of allegations against Trump's original nominee Pete Hegseth.
item.dc:creator: Beth Rousseau, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court hears challenge to law banning gender-affirming care for trans kids
item.description: At issue is a Tennessee law that bans access to hormones, puberty blockers, and other treatments for trans kids in the state.
item.dc:creator: Nina Totenberg, MPR News

item.title: St. Cloud appoints first Somali American to city council
item.description: On a unanimous vote, the city council appointed Hudda Ibrahim to fill a vacancy on the seven-member board. She replaces Jake Anderson, who was elected St. Cloud mayor last month.
item.dc:creator: Kirsti Marohn, MPR News

item.title: Sen. Omar Fateh announces candidacy for Minneapolis mayor, says Frey is ‘failing’ residents
item.description: The first Somali elected to the Minnesota Senate is now seeking to lead the states largest city. Sen. Omar Fateh announced Monday he will challenge incumbent, and fellow DFLer Jacob Frey for the mayor’s office.
item.dc:creator: Cari Spencer, MPR News

item.title: Wave of Democrats slam Biden's pardon of his son Hunter
item.description: While some Democrats extended a measure of sympathy, President Biden has taken heat on several fronts.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump taps investment banker Warren Stephens for UK ambassador
item.description: President-elect Trump announced on Monday that investment banker Warren Stephens would serve as U.S. ambassador to the United Kingdom.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5
